description_long: This is a cylindrical sculpture with a domed top, featuring intricate carvings and reliefs. The upper section displays three seated figures, each housed within an arched alcove beneath a stylized lotus petal canopy. The midsection is adorned with vertical columns of inscribed text, likely in East Asian script, suggesting cultural or religious significance. The lower section is decorated with detailed reliefs of standing human figures, each framed within its own panel. The surface has a dark, aged patina, giving the object a sense of antiquity and reverence. The craftsmanship and iconography suggest it may represent a Buddhist or historical monument.
description: A cylindrical, dark-patinated sculpture with carved figures, inscriptions, and relief panels, topped by a lotus-petal dome. The object features intricate religious or historical iconography.
description_view_0: This view shows three seated figures in alcoves beneath the domed top, with inscribed text and relief panels visible on the lower sections.
description_view_1: Similar to view 0, but the central seated figure is more directly visible, and the relief panels below show different human figures.
description_view_2: This angle highlights the side of the sculpture, revealing more of the inscribed text and a new set of relief figures on the lower section.
description_view_3: This view presents another arrangement of the seated figures and relief panels, with a prominent standing figure in the lower section.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
